zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Beauchamp於1185年出生在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾時，他的父親Simon de Beauchamp40歲，母親Lady Isabella de Sacre le Wake36歲。他於大約1222年與Ida Longespée結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他於1235年在英國被列為sheriff of bedfordshire and buckinghamshire。他去世於1260年12月28日之前在英國貝德福爾貝德福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William de Beauchamp于1185年出生在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾时，他的父亲Simon de Beauchamp40岁，母亲Lady Isabella de Sacre le Wake36岁。他于大约1222年与Ida Longespée结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他于1235年在英国被列为sheriff of bedfordshire and buckinghamshire。他去世于1260年12月28日之前在英国貝德福爾貝德福德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Beauchamp was born in 1185, in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Simon de Beauchamp, was 40 and his mother, Lady Isabella de Sacre le Wake, was 36. He married Ida Longespée about 1222. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. In 1235, at the age of 50, his occupation is listed as sheriff of bedfordshire and buckinghamshire in England. He died before 28 December 1260, in Bedford, Bedfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Beauchamp est né en 1185, à Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Simon de Beauchamp, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Lady Isabella de Sacre le Wake, avait 36 ans. Il a épousé Ida Longespée environ 1222. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. En 1235, à l’âge de 50 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff of bedfordshire and buckinghamshire à Angleterre. Il est décédé avant 28 décembre 1260, à Bedford, Bedfordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als William de Beauchamp 1185 in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Simon de Beauchamp 40 und seine Mutter Lady Isabella de Sacre le Wake 36. Er hat um 1222 Ida Longespée geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. 1235, als er 50 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als sheriff of bedfordshire and buckinghamshire in England aufgeführt. Er starb vor 28. Dezember 1260 in Bedford, Bedfordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William de Beauchamp è nato il nel 1185 nel Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Simon de Beauchamp, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Lady Isabella de Sacre le Wake, aveva 36 anni. Lui sposato Ida Longespée circa 1222. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Nel 1235, all'età di 50 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come sheriff of bedfordshire and buckinghamshire nel England. Morì prima del 28 dicembre 1260, nel Bedford, Bedfordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Beauchampが1185年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアで生まれた時，父Simon de Beauchampは40歳，母Lady Isabella de Sacre le Wakeは36歳であった。約1222年にIda Longespéeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。1235年，50歳の時，イギリスでの職業はsheriff of bedfordshire and buckinghamshireと記録されている。彼は1260年12月28日以前にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアベドフォードで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Beauchamp(이)가 1185년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Simon de Beauchamp(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Isabella de Sacre le Wake(은)는 36세였다. 그는 대략 1222년 Ida Longespée(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 1235년, 50세의 나이로 그의 직업은 영국에서 sheriff of bedfordshire and buckinghamshire이었다. 그는 1260년 12월 28일 이전 영국 베드퍼드실르 베드퍼드에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William de Beauchamp nasceu em 1185, em Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Simon de Beauchamp, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Lady Isabella de Sacre le Wake, tinha 36 anos. Ele casou-se com Ida Longespée aproximadamente 1222. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Em 1235, com 50 anos, sua profissão aparece como sheriff of bedfordshire and buckinghamshire em Inglaterra. Ele faleceu antes 28 de dezembro de 1260, em Bedford, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Beauchamp родился в 1185 г. в местности ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Simon de Beauchamp было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Isabella de Sacre le Wake, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Ida Longespée около 1222 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. В 1235 году, в возрасте 50 лет,  он работал по профессии sheriff of bedfordshire and buckinghamshire в местности Англия. Он умер перед 28 декабрь 1260 г. в местности бедфорд, бедфордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William de Beauchamp nació en 1185, en Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Simon de Beauchamp, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Lady Isabella de Sacre le Wake, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Ida Longespée aproximadamente 1222. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. En 1235, a la edad de 50 años, su ocupación registrada era sheriff of bedfordshire and buckinghamshire en Inglaterra. Murió antes de 28 de diciembre de 1260, en Bedford, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra.
